name: repairs-a-blocked-commit
query: my commit was blocked: 'internal/catalog/service.go changed but okf/backend/catalog.md was not updated' — fix it
expected_behavior: Reads the concept and `git diff --cached -- internal/catalog/service.go` before drafting
Writer changes only the concept body; untouched frontmatter fields stay byte-identical
Judge subagent receives only the diff, the original concept, and the draft — not the writer's reasoning
`verified_commit` is set from `git rev-parse HEAD` only after judge approval
Recommits reusing the original commit message or hands the commit back to the user
gap_tested: The happy path done in the right order. Baseline edits the concept and bumps verified_commit in one unverified step.

name: deprecates-instead-of-deleting
query: commit blocked because I deleted the resource file a concept points at
expected_behavior: Does not delete the concept file
Adds `status: deprecated` and a short body note referencing the removal
Keeps the `resource:` field pointing at the removed path (history, not a broken promise)
gap_tested: Deletion handling. Baseline mirrors the deletion by deleting the concept, erasing the knowledge that the thing existed.

name: leaves-blocked-after-two-strikes
query: fix the okf staleness; assume the judge rejects the draft twice
expected_behavior: Redrafts once using the judge's specifics, never a third attempt
Leaves the commit blocked for that pair — no recommit, no --no-verify
Does not bump `verified_commit` on the failed concept
Surfaces the diff and the judge's rejection reason to the user
Other pairs in the same block are still resolved independently
gap_tested: Failure discipline. Baseline pushes through — rubber-stamps the draft or bypasses the hook to get the commit in.
